ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
‼️and it’s a wild story👇🏻

🤍 Follow for more unique places around the world @tripuntraveled

this looks like one of the most magical towns in Italy but the famous cone-shaped houses here weren’t originally built to impress

they were constructed using a technique that allowed them to be dismantled quickly whenever tax inspectors came through the region

Centuries later, what started as a clever workaround became one of the most unique towns in Europe and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

If you’re road-tripping through Puglia, this place is absolutely worth adding to your itinerary.

This is…

📍 Alberobello, Puglia, Italy

🚗 How to get here

Alberobello is easiest to visit by car as part of a Puglia road trip.

From Bari, it’s about a 1-hour drive. From Polignano a Mare, Monopoli, or Ostuni, it’s around 30–45 minutes.

If you don’t have a car, take a train from Bari to Alberobello with a transfer, or use a direct bus from Bari.

🅿️ There are several paid parking lots just outside the historic center, then it’s a short walk into town.

Save this for your Italy trip

🚗 How to get here
Fly into Vigo Airport or drive from Santiago de Compostela in about an hour.
Ferries also connect the city to the islands offshore.

🌊 What to do
Explore the historic old town
Walk up to Castro Fortress for panoramic views
Walk along the marina
Take a ferry to the legendary Islands of the Gods
Watch sunset over the estuary

🦪 What to eat
Fresh oysters straight from the bay
Galician octopus
Empanada gallega
Albariño wine

👟 What to wear
Comfortable shoes because the city is surprisingly steep

🕗 Best time to visit
Late spring through early fall when the islands are easiest to access

This is…

📍 Vigo, Galicia, Spain

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️if you are visiting Venice this year👇🏻 

🤍 Follow for more unique places and travel guides around the world

Getting there

Fly into Venice Marco Polo Airport. From there, you can take a water taxi (expensive but iconic) or the Alilaguna water bus (budget-friendly). 

If you’re coming by train, Venezia Santa Lucia drops you straight into the heart of the city.

Where to stay

If it’s your first time, stay near San Marco or along the Grand Canal so you can walk everywhere early in the morning before crowds take over.

🤍 I stayed at the @stregisvenice and it was an epic experience. One of my favorite moments of staying here was that every evening they carry on a ritual called champagne sabering - where a bottle is opened with a sword (yes, an actual sword) and I actually got to do it myself and then try it 🤍

Things to do

Get lost. Not even kidding! Venice is best experienced with no plan
Wake up early and walk through Piazza San Marco before 8 AM
Take a vaporetto or gondola ride down the Grand Canal
Visit Burano for colorful houses or Murano for glassmaking
Climb the Campanile for one of the best views in the city

What to eat

Skip the tourist places right by the main squares.
Walk a few streets away and look for smaller, local spots.
Try cicchetti (Venetian tapas), fresh pasta, and tiramisu (this is where it was born).
Also, don’t leave without having a spritz by the water.

Things no one tells you ⚠️

Venice is beautiful, but it’s also crowded. Early mornings and late evenings are your best friend
Restaurants near major attractions are often overpriced and not worth it
Pack light! There are a LOT of bridges and no Ubers here
Acqua alta (flooding) can happen, especially in certain months, so check ahead

📌 Save this for your Italy trip

__

© @tripuntraveled
All rights reserved.

caption:
‼️ Don’t skip this EPIC border town 🤯

🤍 Follow for more unique places around the world @tripuntraveled 

🚗 How to get here
No direct trains go here. Take a bus from San Sebastián (30 mins) or rent a car.
If you are coming from France, take the train or bus to Hendaye, then a quick ferry across the Bidasoa River 🚤.
Park outside the walled old town or near the Marina.

🌸 What to do
Explore the medieval old town along Calle Mayor
Look at the red, green & blue Basque balconies covered in flowers
Watch sunset from the fortress walls or Parador terrace
Hop on the ferry to Hendaye, France for a very cool experience

🍷 What to eat
Pintxos crawl in the Marina neighborhood
Try a glass of txakoli (the Basque white wine)

👟 What to wear
Comfy shoes because cobblestone streets

🕗 Best time to visit
Early morning or evening because the colors look magical

This is…

📍 Hondarribia, Basque Country, Spain

📌 Save this if you love colorful towns!

Would you take the ferry to France… or stay here forever? 👀

ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
‼️and the alleyways look like a museum 🤯

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

 Amalfi coast had colorful villages, lemon groves, and sea views.

But in this town, I found myself in the alleyways staring at doors, walls, staircases, and tiny lovely details.

This town is famous for its ceramics and instead of keeping the art inside, they have brought it out to the streets.

This is…

📍 Vietri sul Mare, Amalfi Coast, Italy

🚗 How to get here

Take a train to Salerno and then a short train, bus, or taxi from there. If you’re driving the Amalfi Coast, it’s the first town you’ll reach from Salerno.

Things to know
Vietri sul Mare is considered the ceramic capital of the Amalfi Coast
some of the best ceramic artwork is hidden in the alleyways
it’s significantly less crowded than Positano and Amalfi

📌 Save this for your Amalfi Coast trip

What’s the most unexpected thing you’ve ended up seeing while traveling?

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️location👇🏻

🤍 follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

I kept spotting these little green donkeys everywhere. On rooftops, ceramic shops, hidden on walls, even watching over the streets.

Turns out they’re one of the town’s most beloved symbols and a reminder of the ceramic traditions that have shaped the Amalfi coast for generations.

The tradition dates back nearly a century when a German artist created a colorful ceramic donkey that unexpectedly became the symbol of the town and a sign of good luck.

This is…

📍 Vietri sul Mare, Amalfi Coast, Italy

🚗 How to get here

Take a train to Salerno and then a short train, bus, or taxi ride from there. If you’re driving the Amalfi Coast, this is usually the first town you’ll reach.

Things to know

It’s famous for hand painted ceramics and is considered the ceramic capital of the Amalfi Coast

The best souvenir to bring home is locally made pottery rather than the mass produced items you’ll find elsewhere

Spend time exploring the side streets because that’s where you’ll find many of the ceramic installations and hidden details

📌 Save this for your Italy trip because this one is worth it.

Did you know about the green donkey?

ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
I never grew up thinking, “One day, I’ll be a content creator.” 

👩‍💻I was supposed to follow the safe path.
I went to school, got my degree, landed my 9-5 job in the advanced air mobility industry and I’m VERY proud of that. I get to be part of very few people shaping the future of air mobility. 

But deep down… I always felt this quiet pull towards something more. I always find myself chasing little pockets of the world that most people skip or haven’t even heard of! The places not trending on Instagram and tiny villages where you get lost on purpose.

So I picked up my camera 📸 and started sharing stories through not just places, but emotions. Slowly, my small corner of Instagram grew into a beautiful community of people who are always looking for something unique or different.

✅ I want to be a content creator to inspire 
people who feel stuck, 
people who think travel isn’t for them,
people who think they need to quit their jobs to see the world. 
You don’t. I didn’t.
✅ You can chase your dreams without sacrificing stability.
✅ You can start before you’re “ready.”
‼️ You can live fully even if the world tells you to choose one path.

It’s possible to build a life where one day, I can turn this into something even bigger ✈️ my own business, my own brand, my own freedom. Remember, “you don’t need to be great to start but you NEED TO start in order to be great”. 

and if one person watches my content and thinks “Wait… maybe I can do this too”..that’s the real win

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️and has one of the strangest buildings in Europe 🤯

🤍 Follow for more unique places around the world @tripuntraveled

This place is known as the birthplace of Salvador Dalí, but the town itself feels like one of his creations.

The Dalí Theatre Museum is one of the strangest places in Europe. Giant eggs on the roof, golden sculptures on the walls, hidden illusions around every corner, and Dalí himself is buried beneath.

Even if you’re not into art, this place makes you question what’s real and what’s part of the performance.

This is…

📍 Figueres, Catalonia, Spain

🚗 How to get here
Figueres is about 1 hour from Barcelona by high speed train or 90 minutes by car. It’s an easy day trip or stop on a Costa Brava road trip.

What to eat
Try suquet de peix (Catalan fish stew), local cured meats, and crema catalana for dessert.

📌 Save this for your next Spain trip

have you seen this building before?

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
This fairytale town has heart shaped cutouts on the windows…and centuries ago, they weren’t just for decoration.

In medieval times, a heart carved into the wooden shutters meant a girl of marriageable age lived in that house.

No heart usually meant the “listing” was taken or the family wasn’t open to offers 🤫

This is…

📍 Colmar, France

Save this if you’re single and booking flights immediately 😅😂

ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
‼️things to know before visiting italy 👇🏻

🤍 follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

after roadtripping across italy (except for Sicily and the Dolomites), these are the things i wish more people knew

1. do not drive into historic centers blindly…many have ztl zones with cameras and fines. park outside and walk in.

2. italy is not one trip…tuscany, puglia, amalfi, rome, umbria, and the lakes all feel completely different.

3. rent a car for villages, not big cities…a car is amazing for countryside roadtrips, but stressful and expensive in rome, florence, naples, and venice.

4. regional train tickets may need validation…especially paper tickets. buying the ticket is not always enough.

5. the best places are between the famous ones…tiny hill towns, monasteries, viewpoints, and random stops were some of my favorite memories.

6. restaurants may close in the afternoon, always check hours before driving somewhere just to eat.

7. coperto is normal…that small charge on your bill is usually a cutlery, table/cover charge, not a scam.

8. roadtrips take longer than google says…parking, tolls, tiny roads, photo stops, and mountain drives add up fast.

9. shoulder or winter season is the secret. peak summer can make even magical places feel exhausting.

10. learn these words
parcheggio = parking
chiuso = closed
aperto = open
uscita = exit

italy is beautiful, colorful, chaotic, dramatic, and absolutely worth it.

📌 save this before your italy trip

what surprised you most about visiting italy?

ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
‼️location and details👇🏻

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

This underground network was carved into volcanic rock over 2,000 years ago

The tunnels were first created by the ancient Greeks, later expanded by the Romans into aqueducts, and eventually used as bomb shelters during WWII where thousands of people hid and survived underground for months.

Walking through it honestly feels unreal.

There are massive hidden chambers, ancient tunnels, underground water systems, passageways, and even extremely narrow sections where you have to squeeze sideways through the walls

This is…

📍Napoli Sotterranea, Naples, Italy

Tour time is around 1.5–2 hours
Wear comfortable shoes because parts can be slippery and tight
You have to go down and climb up a lot of stairs

Save this for your Italy trip

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️location👇🏻

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled 

This cliffside estate is hidden high above Ravello, and filled with winding pathways, ancient temples, rose gardens, marble busts, and views that will leave you speechless.

The highlight is the famous terrace of infinity…it makes you feel like you’re standing at the edge of the world with nothing but sea, sky, and cliffs stretching endlessly in front of you.

This is…

📍 Villa Cimbrone, Ravello, Italy

How to get here -

If you are visiting by car, park in Ravello and walk about 10-15 minutes through the historic center to reach the villa entrance.

Things to see here…
Terrace of Infinity
Cloister
Temple of Bacchus
Rose Garden
Crypt
Panoramic Amalfi Coast viewpoints

Visit early in the morning, near sunset, or in winter for the most peaceful experience.

ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
‼️location and details👇🏻

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled 

At first glance, this place looks like a cathedral or palace…but it was actually built for silk merchants and businessmen.

The main trading hall is one of the most beautiful interiors I’ve seen in Spain with giant spiraling columns stretching toward the ceiling like palm trees or vines.

The architecture was intentionally designed to impress, intimidate, and show off the city’s wealth to foreign traders arriving here in the 1400s.

and there was even a prison built into the complex for merchants who couldn’t pay their debts

This is

📍La Lonja de la Seda, Valencia, Spain

Things to know
it was built in the late 15th century during Valencia’s silk boom
it’s a UNESCO World Heritage Site
it’s one of the greatest examples of Gothic civil architecture in Europe

Save this for your Spain trip

Have you ever seen a trading hall this dramatic before?

walk through all 9 themed gardens

explore the pink salons and art collections

caption:
‼️cleaner than most people’s swimming pools 🤯👇🏻

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

This alpine town is so clean and blue that people call it the Venice of the Alps

one of the cleanest lakes in Europe, surrounded by canals, pastel buildings, flower-covered bridges, and dramatic Alpine mountains that look painted into the background.

You can spend the morning wandering through the old town, eating fresh croissants beside the canals, then rent a bike and ride around the lake with views that genuinely do not stop.

Things to do here
• Walk through Vieille Ville (Old Town)
• Cross Pont des Amours
• Bike around Lake Annecy
• Take a boat cruise on the lake
• Visit Château d’Annecy
• Try raclette, tartiflette, and fresh Alpine cheese
• Watch sunset from the lake promenade

This is…

📍Annecy, France

📌 Save this for your France / Alps trip

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️yes it’s true 🤯 

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled

For centuries, the Doge of Venice would sail out into the lagoon on a ceremonial boat and throw a ring into the Adriatic Sea.

This was called the marriage of the sea or Sposalizio del Mare.

But this wasn’t just a romantic fairytale. The sea made Venice rich, the sea protected Venice, and the sea connected Venice to the world.

So every year, the city renewed its bond with the water by symbolically marrying it with a ring.

The tradition is still alive today during Festa della Sensa.

📍 Venice, Italy

📌 Save this for your next Venice trip because this city has way more stories than gondolas, canals, and pretty views.

Would you attend a wedding ceremony with the sea?

ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
‼️location and itinerary details👇🏻

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled 

Most people stay in Barcelona, but roadtripping through Catalonia completely changed the trip for me.

Here’s the exact 3-day itinerary ⬇️

Day 1 — Girona
Colorful river houses, cathedral views, the Jewish Quarter, and sunset from the city walls.

Day 2 Morning — Besalú
A tiny medieval town with one of the prettiest stone bridges in Spain.

Day 2 Afternoon — Cadaqués
A whitewashed coastal town that honestly feels more like Greece than Spain.

Day 3 Morning — Begur
Castle ruins, sea views, beach cafés, and relaxed coastal vibes.

Day 3 Late Morning — Peratallada
Stone streets, ivy-covered walls, quiet plazas, and hidden restaurants.

Day 3 Afternoon — Pals
Medieval towers, arches, countryside views, and gelato stops.

Day 3 Evening — Palafrugell / Llafranc
Sunset by the beach, tapas, and a beautiful coastal walk.

Best time to visit
Spring and Fall are perfect
Summer is beautiful too, but VERY hot.

📌 Save this for your Spain roadtrip

This tiny medieval village completely pulled me out of reality for a few hours.

The cobblestones polished by centuries of footsteps, flower boxes were spilling out of wooden windows, and it felt like I was in a storybook.

You can hear birds echoing through the alleyways, forks clinking against plates from hidden restaurants, and the smell of melted butter and fresh galettes drifting through the streets.

This is…

📍 Pérouges, France

🚗 How to get here
Pérouges is about 40 minutes from Lyon by car. You can also take a train to Meximieux and grab a short taxi ride to the village.

What to eat
The famous galette au sucre is a must here. It’s a crispy buttery sugar tart that basically perfumes the entire village.

📌 Save this for your France trip

ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
Utah really is one of the most stunning places in the entire country!! 😍

If you are planning a trip to this amazing part of the USA, make sure to follow @peekinourjournal and see more bucketlist spots and destinations to have the best time out here ❤️

↩️ Arches National Park- One of the most famous national parks in the country. It boasts amazing natural arches, including Delicate Arch which is Utah’s License Plate Symbol
♦️ Canyonlands National Park- Spectacular park with amazing views! Catching the sunrise at Mesa Arch when it glows red, has to be on your list
🐎 Dead Horse Point State Park- One of the best sunrise spots in Moab, this state park deserves so much more love! The entry fee is $20 and we highly recommend visiting this place on your trip to Moab
🛻 Marlboro Point- This hidden gem is only accessible via a 4x4 vehicle and offers jaw dropping views across deep, rugged canyons
🏜️ Goblin Valley State Park- Red rocks and rugged hoodoos dominate the landscape of this beautiful park. You can purchase a drone permit at the entrance station 
👽 Bentonite Hills- Located near Mars Research station, this otherworldly spot will surely make you feel like you transported to another planet
🏰 Capitol Reef National Park- The least visited National Park in Utah, this destination is a hidden gem.There are stunning views everywhere and park offers some amazing hikes and even world class rappelling!
🪨 Factory Butte- This beautiful monolith overlooks Hanksville and can be seen from the highway. We suggest off roading and getting closer to it as the landscape is just surreal 
🌒 Moon Overlook- Otherworldly landscape which looks so much like the surface of the Moon. Best accessed via a 4x4, this spot is located close to Hanksville

.
.
.
.
.

#utah #visitutah #moab #hanksvilleutah #utahroadtrip

ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
‼️ and most people have never heard of this place 🤯

🤍 Follow for more unique places around the world @tripuntraveled

The Decameron by Giovanni Boccaccio was written right here in this tiny Tuscan hilltop town during the Black Death in 1353. 

This earthy realism book is a collection of 100 scandalous stories and tales that were so outrageous it got banned across countries.

Boccaccio later inspired Shakespeare, Chaucer, and now Netflix.

This is…

📍 Certaldo Alto, Tuscany, Italy

🚗 How to get here
It is easily accessible by car or you can take a train to Certaldo. Certaldo Alto sits on top of Certaldo Basso so it’s a steep walk uphill or you can take a funicular.

🧁 What to eat
Local Certaldo red onion dishes are a must try here. They even have ice cream in that flavor

📌 Save this for your Tuscany trip because this one is worth it.

Did you know The Decameron was written here?

musicInfo.artist_name: Mark Keali'i Ho'omalu, Kamehameha Schools Children's Chorus

caption:
‼️One of the prettiest roadtrips in France👇🏻

🤍 Follow @tripuntraveled for more unique places and roadtrips around the world

Most people go to Paris, Nice, or the lavender fields…

but this route through eastern France honestly felt like stepping through 3 completely different worlds in a single trip 🤯

🏡 Colmar felt like a real-life fairytale with colorful half-timbered houses, flower-covered canals, tiny wine bars, and pretty streets. It’s also one of the places believed to have inspired Beauty and the Beast.

🏔️ Annecy is called the Venice of the Alps and the water here is so insanely turquoise that it barely looks real. Rent a bike around the lake, walk the canals at sunset, and don’t skip the mountain viewpoints around the area.

🕯️Pérouges felt like traveling straight back to the Middle Ages. Cobblestone streets, ivy-covered stone buildings, wooden doors, candlelit restaurants…even film crews use it because it barely looks touched by modern life.

This route is perfect if you love
fairytale towns
scenic roadtrips
slow mornings and long lunches
medieval villages
mountain lakes
cozy cafés and bakeries
places that still feel authentic

Suggested route
Fly into Lyon → Pérouges → Annecy → Colmar. You can fly out of Paris or another nearby airport.

Best time to visit
May–June or September–October for fewer crowds and beautiful weather. December is magical too if you want Christmas markets in Colmar

Don’t miss
tarte flambée and Alsatian wine in Colmar
raclette/fondue near Annecy
galette de Pérouges (their famous sugar tart)

Save this for your future France trip 🇫🇷

© @tripuntraveled

ownerFullName: 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐬𝐡𝐭𝐢 | Road Trips & Unique Places

caption:
‼️location and details👇🏻

🤍 Follow for more unique places and roadtrips around the world @tripuntraveled 

This city is known for its chaos…the traffic, the noise, and all the hustle and bustle

but then there is this peaceful monastery covered in 300 year old hand painted tiles that tell stories of everyday life in Italy.

You’ll see farmers, villages, gardens, landscapes, and scenes that will draw you in.

These are wrapped around the benches and columns throughout the cloister, turning an entire garden into a work of art.

This is…

📍 Santa Chiara Cloister, Naples, Italy

How to get here
Fly into Naples International Airport and take a taxi or use public transportation into the historic center. The monastery is an easy stop while exploring the city.

Don’t miss
famous majolica-tiled cloister
peaceful gardens
Basilica of Santa Chiara
archaeological remains beneath the complex

📌 Save this for your Naples itinerary because it’s one of the most beautiful places in the city

Have you ever come across stories being told through tiles?

ownerFullName: PALAK & VAIBHAV | Travel and Adventure Couple

caption:
‼️ and it’s literally built on the cliff edge

🤍 follow for more unique places around the world @tripuntraveled

🚗 How to get here
No direct trains go here…rent a car or take a day trip from Málaga (1h45m) or Seville (2h).
Park near the historic center and walk

🌉 What to do
Walk across Puente Nuevo which is a massive bridge connecting two cliffs
Look down into El Tajo gorge
Watch the sunset from Alameda Park
Stay overnight to see it without the tour bus crowds

🍷 What to eat
Tapas with a view at a cliffside restaurant
Local wine
Tortilla de patatas

👟 What to wear
Comfy shoes with grip

🕗 Best time to visit
Golden hour or early morning.. mid-day is packed with tour groups
Walk down to the base of the bridge for the best photos

This is…

📍Ronda, Andalucía, Spain

📌 Save this for your Spain trip

caption:

🤍 Follow @tripuntraveled for more unique places and roadtrips around the world

There are very few places where literally every direction feels unreal

pastel buildings and tiny fishing boats, turquoise water, hidden coves, pine-covered cliffs, luxury yachts, and old stone paths that somehow keep leading to even crazier views.

This is…

📍Portofino, Italy

Best viewpoints in Portofino
Castello Brown
Faro di Portofino
Chiesa di San Giorgio
The trail to San Fruttuoso
Belvedere lungo the cliff paths
The walk toward Paraggi Bay

Things to know
Wear comfortable shoes because Portofino is WAY more vertical than it looks
Early morning and golden hour completely transform the colors here

Save this for your Italy trip

caption:
Alaska is one of the most pristine, wild and untouched places in USA and the summer months bring a new leaf of life 🚞

✨ For more such content with bucketlist destinations and epic wildlife, follow @peekinourjournal and get the most amazing ideas for your next trip 🫶

🐻 The best time to explore Alaska to its fullest is from June to August with July being the busiest month, and for good reason 🙌🏻

👉🏻 If you’re a first timer, we recommend these 5 experiences to get the best bang for your buck:

🫎 Denali National Park
🛥️ Seward

caption:

✨Hi! We’re Palak and Vaibhav, a couple based in eastern USA who loves to travel and enjoy the beautiful outdoors. Follow along our journey if you like to hike, go on roadtrips and travel the world with your partner ♥️

🇮🇸 Iceland is one of the most beautiful countries in the world and in the summer months, you can witness the midnight sun. During this time there is always some sunlight and it never gets completely dark 🤯

☀️ It leaves behind the beautiful golden glow of the sun and the stunning landscapes of this country look even more magical 🥹
